Items.Title: 用高阶函数整理getServerSideProps(Server-SideProps) | Tidying up getServerSideProps(Server-Side Rendering) with Higher Order Functions
Items.Description:
我们都去过那里； 一旦我们的项目变得更大， getServerSideProps就开始变得难以管理。 因为我们必须处理缓存、数据获取、身份验证检查和数据格式化以降低客户端的复杂性。
发生这种情况时，会大大降低开发人员体验（DX）； 出于这个原因，开发人员需要四处寻找他们想要的代码。
当您第一次看到一个大的getServerSideProps时，您可能已经听说过类似的内容 - “缓存头在哪里”或“我们在哪里进行身份验证检查”。

现在，您可能会想 - 如果您只是将一些代码提取到有意义的函数中，这些都很容易躲避。
你是对的。 可以这样做，但它们会使您的getServerSideProps非常密集和冗长。

让我们仔细看看这里的代码：

 export async function getServerSideProps ({ req , res }) { const userIsAuthenticated = req . auth . user . id ; if ( ! userIsAuthenticated ) { return { redirect : { permanent : false , destination : ' /world-without-cookie ' , }, }; } /* Big logging section */ /* Check if the data available in redis or memcache */ /* If not get data from Db */ /* Format data for the client */ res . setHeader ( ' Cache-Control ' , ' public, s-maxage=10, stale-while-revalidate=59 ' ); return { props : { time : new Date (). toISOString (), }, }; }

我们可以把它变成这样：

 export async function getServerSideProps ({ req , res }) { bounceUsers ( req ); logStuff ( req ); const cacheData = getCacheData ( req ); const dbData = getDataFromDb (); const formatData = formatDataForClient ( cacheData , dbData ); res . setHeader ( ' Cache-Control ' , ' public, s-maxage=10, stale-while-revalidate=59 ' ); return { props : { data : formatData , }, }; }

在这种情况下，我会选择后者。 但总有改进的余地。 我们仍然可以通过将代码分成单独的高阶函数来提高代码的可读性。
因此，函数将只知道它们需要的部分，开发人员不必四处寻找代码，并且能够在眨眼之间找到他们需要的代码块。

让我们将代码重构为更实用的方式：

 type Options = { kickUser : boolean ; }; type InjectedProps = { userLocation : string ; }; type GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps = GetServerSidePropsContext & InjectedProps ; type Callback = ( context : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps , ) => Promise < GetServerSidePropsResult < any >> ;

如果您正在使用 Typescript，您首先需要添加几个类型以使其与 TS 一起使用。 不要担心InjectedProps和Options ，现在，一会儿就会到达那里。

 export async function getServerSideProps ({ req , res }) { const userIsAuthenticated = req . auth . user . id ; if ( ! userIsAuthenticated ) { return { redirect : { permanent : false , destination : ' /world-without-cookie ' , }, }; } return { props : { time : new Date (). toISOString (), }, }; }

让我们首先把它变成一个高阶函数。

 export const bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet = ( cb : Callback , options ?: Options ) => { return async ( ctx : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { const userIsAuthenticated = ctx . req . auth . user . id ; if ( options ?. kickUser && userIsAuthenticated ) { return { redirect : { permanent : false , destination : ' /world-without-cookie ' , }, }; } const getUserLocation = await magicalLocationFinderFunction (); const injectedContext = { ... ctx , userLocation : getUserLocation (), }; return cb ?.( injectedContext ) || {}; }; }; const getServerSideHomeProps = async ( context : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { return { props : { message : ' Me cookie monster. That all there is to it. Me love to eat cookie. Sometimes eat whole, sometimes me chew it. ' , }, }; }; export const getServerSideProps = bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet ( getServerSideHomeProps , { kickUser : false , });

如果您熟悉高阶函数 (HOC) 的概念，那对您来说应该不会那么陌生。 本质上，我们所做的只是用bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet包装我们的getServerSideHomeProps 。

因此，当我们收到请求时，我们将首先检查用户是否以我们想要的方式进行了身份验证； 如果是这样，我们将检查我们传递kickUser的参数。 然后，如果所有条件都满足，我们会让用户进入getServerSideHomeProps并向客户端传递消息。 我们还在上下文中注入了额外的信息来查看用户的位置。 我们可以很容易地用这种方法丰富我们的上下文。

让我们配置缓存头和数据获取。

 type Options = Partial < { kickUser : boolean ; cacheMaxAge : number ; } > ; export const bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet = ( cb : Callback , options ?: Options ) => { return async ( ctx : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { const userIsAuthenticated = ctx . req . auth . user . id ; if ( options ?. kickUser && ! userIsAuthenticated ) { return { redirect : { permanent : false , destination : ' /world-without-cookie ' , }, }; } const getUserLocation = await magicalLocationFinderFunction (); const injectedContext = { ... ctx , userLocation : getUserLocation (), }; return cb ?.( injectedContext ) || {}; }; }; export const setCacheHeaders = ( cb : Callback , options ?: Options ) => { return async ( ctx : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { ctx . res . setHeader ( ' Cache-Control ' , `public, s-maxage= ${ options ?. cacheMaxAge } , stale-while-revalidate=59` , ); return cb ?.( ctx ) || {}; }; }; export const fetchData = ( cb : Callback , options ?: Options ) => { return async ( ctx : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { const formattedData = await getDataFromDbOrCache (); const injectedContext = { ... ctx , formattedData , }; return cb ?.( injectedContext ) || {}; }; }; const getServerSideHomeProps = async ( context : GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps ) => { const formattedData = context . formattedData ; return { props : { message : formattedData ?? ' Me cookie monster. That all there is to it. Me love to eat cookie. Sometimes eat whole, sometimes me chew it. ' , }, }; }; export const getServerSideProps = bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet ( setCacheHeaders ( fetchData ( getServerSideHomeProps ), { cacheMaxAge : 1000 }), { kickUser : false , }, );

如您所见，此模式可以根据您的需要轻松扩展。 而且，通过这种模式，我们可以轻松避开臃肿的getServerSideHomeProps 。

这种模式的优点

  • 可以轻松扩展
  • 原子
  • 比常规getServerSideProps更具声明性
  • 更容易调试
  • 包装器可以很容易地重复使用

这种模式的缺点

  • 乍一看可能很难理解

利大于弊。 但是，如果您的需求不如这些复杂，请不要费心将这种模式实现到每个 SSR 函数中。 不要忘记保持简单。

何塞·巴勃罗·拉米雷斯·巴尔加斯 - 一旦暂停，ogzhanolguncu 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

原文:

We've all been there; once our project gets bigger, getServerSideProps starts to become a pain in the ass to manage. Because we have to deal with caching, data-fetching, auth checks, and data formatting to decrease the complexity of the client.
The size of the getServerSideProps gets considerably big.

When this happens, it reduces Developer Experience(DX) highly; for that reason, developers need to hop around to find the code they want.
You might have heard something like this when you first saw a big getServerSideProps - "Where the hell is the cache headers" or "Where do we do the auth checks".

Now, you might be thinking - These are all pretty easy to dodge if you just extract some code into meaningful functions.
And you are right. It is possible to do that, but they will make your getServerSideProps very dense and verbose.

Let's take a closer look at code here:

export async function getServerSideProps({ req, res }) { const userIsAuthenticated = req.auth.user.id; if (!userIsAuthenticated) { return { redirect: { permanent: false, destination: '/world-without-cookie', }, }; } /* Big logging section */ /* Check if the data available in redis or memcache */ /* If not get data from Db */ /* Format data for the client */ res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'public, s-maxage=10, stale-while-revalidate=59'); return { props: { time: new Date().toISOString(), }, };
}

We can turn this into this:

export async function getServerSideProps({ req, res }) { bounceUsers(req); logStuff(req); const cacheData = getCacheData(req); const dbData = getDataFromDb(); const formatData = formatDataForClient(cacheData, dbData); res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'public, s-maxage=10, stale-while-revalidate=59'); return { props: { data: formatData, }, };
}

I would pick the latter in this case. But there is always room for improvement. We can still increase the readability of our code by separating it into individual higher-order functions.
Therefore, functions will be aware of only the parts they require, and developers won't have to hop around to spot the code and will be able to locate the chunk of code they need in a blink of an eye.

Let's refactor our code into a more functional way:

type Options = { kickUser: boolean;
}; type InjectedProps = { userLocation: string;
}; type GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps = GetServerSidePropsContext & InjectedProps; type Callback = ( context: GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps,
) => Promise<GetServerSidePropsResult<any>>;

If you are working with Typescript, you first need to add a couple of types to make it work with TS. Don't worry about InjectedProps and Options, for now, will get there in a moment.

export async function getServerSideProps({ req, res }) { const userIsAuthenticated = req.auth.user.id; if (!userIsAuthenticated) { return { redirect: { permanent: false, destination: '/world-without-cookie', }, }; } return { props: { time: new Date().toISOString(), }, };
}

Let's first turn this into a Higher Order Function.

export const bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet = (cb: Callback, options?: Options) => { return async (ctx: GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps) => { const userIsAuthenticated = ctx.req.auth.user.id; if (options?.kickUser && userIsAuthenticated) { return { redirect: { permanent: false, destination: '/world-without-cookie', }, }; } const getUserLocation = await magicalLocationFinderFunction(); const injectedContext = { ...ctx, userLocation: getUserLocation(), }; return cb?.(injectedContext) || {}; };
}; const getServerSideHomeProps = async (context: GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps) => { return { props: { message: 'Me cookie monster. That all there is to it. Me love to eat cookie. Sometimes eat whole, sometimes me chew it.', }, };
}; export const getServerSideProps = bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet(getServerSideHomeProps, { kickUser: false,
});

If you are familiar with the concept of Higher Order Functions(HOC), that shouldn't look so strange to you. In the essence all we did was to wrap our getServerSideHomeProps with bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet.

So when we get a request, we'll first check if the user is authenticated in the way we wanted; if so, we'll check the argument we've passed kickUser. Then, if all the conditions are met, we will let the user move into the getServerSideHomeProps and pass a message to the client. And we also injected additional info into our context to see users' locations. We can easily enrich our context with this method.

Let's configure cache headers and data fetching.

type Options = Partial<{ kickUser: boolean; cacheMaxAge: number;
}>; export const bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet = (cb: Callback, options?: Options) => { return async (ctx: GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps) => { const userIsAuthenticated = ctx.req.auth.user.id; if (options?.kickUser && !userIsAuthenticated) { return { redirect: { permanent: false, destination: '/world-without-cookie', }, }; } const getUserLocation = await magicalLocationFinderFunction(); const injectedContext = { ...ctx, userLocation: getUserLocation(), }; return cb?.(injectedContext) || {}; };
}; export const setCacheHeaders = (cb: Callback, options?: Options) => { return async (ctx: GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps) => { ctx.res.setHeader( 'Cache-Control', `public, s-maxage=${options?.cacheMaxAge}, stale-while-revalidate=59`, ); return cb?.(ctx) || {}; };
}; export const fetchData = (cb: Callback, options?: Options) => { return async (ctx: GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps) => { const formattedData = await getDataFromDbOrCache(); const injectedContext = { ...ctx, formattedData, }; return cb?.(injectedContext) || {}; };
}; const getServerSideHomeProps = async (context: GetServerSidePropsWithInjectedProps) => { const formattedData = context.formattedData; return { props: { message: formattedData ?? 'Me cookie monster. That all there is to it. Me love to eat cookie. Sometimes eat whole, sometimes me chew it.', }, };
}; export const getServerSideProps = bounceUsersIfCredentialsHaveNotMet( setCacheHeaders(fetchData(getServerSideHomeProps), { cacheMaxAge: 1000 }), { kickUser: false, },
);

As you can see this pattern can easily scale with your needs. And, through this pattern we can easily evade bloated getServerSideHomeProps.

Pros of this pattern

  • Can easily scale
  • Atomic
  • More declarative than regular getServerSideProps
  • Easier to debug
  • Wrappers can be easily reused

Cons of this pattern

  • Might be hard to understand at the first look

The pros outweigh the cons. But, still, if your needs are not as sophisticated as these, don't bother to implement this pattern into every single SSR function. Don't forget to keep it simple.

Items.Title: Cookie 和会话 | Cookies and Sessions
Items.Description:
阿巴灵顿

发表于由于 cookie 是大多数 Web 应用程序的重要组成部分，Rails 对内置的 cookie 和会话提供了出色的支持。对我们来说不幸的是，当您使用 rails new appname --api 在 API 模式下创建一个新应用程序时，需要使用的代码默认情况下不包括控制器中的会话和 cookie。

要重新添加会话和 cookie 支持，我们需要在 config/application.rb 文件中更新应用程序的配置：

 # config/application.rb module MyApp class Application < Rails::Application config.load_defaults 6.1 # This is set in apps generated with the --api flag, and removes session/cookie middleware config.api_only = true # Must add these lines! # Adding back cookies and session middleware config.middleware.use ActionDispatch::Cookies config.middleware.use ActionDispatch::Session::CookieStore # Use SameSite=Strict for all cookies to help protect against CSRF config.action_dispatch.cookies_same_site_protection = :strict end end

这会将必要的中间件链接添加到外部站点。 用于在我们的应用程序中使用会话和 cookie。

要访问控制器中的 cookie 哈希，我们还需要在 ApplicationController 中包含 ActionController::Cookies 模块：

 # app/controllers/application_controller.rb class ApplicationController < ActionController::API include ActionController::Cookies end

原文:

arbarrington

Posted on Oct 20

Since cookies are such an important part of most web applications, Rails has excellent support for cookies and sessions baked in. Unfortunately for us, when you create a new application in API mode with rails new appname --api, the code needed for working with sessions and cookies in the controller is excluded by default.

To add session and cookie support back in, we need to update our application's configuration in the config/application.rb file:

# config/application.rb
module MyApp
  class Application < Rails::Application
    config.load_defaults 6.1
    # This is set in apps generated with the --api flag, and removes session/cookie middleware
    config.api_only = true

    # Must add these lines!
    # Adding back cookies and session middleware
    config.middleware.use ActionDispatch::Cookies
    config.middleware.use ActionDispatch::Session::CookieStore

    # Use SameSite=Strict for all cookies to help protect against CSRF
    config.action_dispatch.cookies_same_site_protection = :strict
  end
end

This will add in the necessary middlewareLinks to an external site. for working with sessions and cookies in our application.

To access the cookies hash in our controllers, we also need to include the ActionController::Cookies module in our ApplicationController:

# app/controllers/application_controller.rb
class ApplicationController < ActionController::API
  include ActionController::Cookies
end

Items.Title: 介绍 CodeSandbox GitHub 应用程序 | Introducing the CodeSandbox GitHub App
Items.Description:
我们宣布推出 CodeSandbox GitHub 应用程序，它为您打开的每个拉取请求提供实时开发环境，并支持 Docker 和数据库。

还记得上次有人要求你审查 PR 是什么时候吗？ 您可能不得不提交您当前的工作，切换您的整个上下文并旋转所有内容，以便您可以查看它。 然后，执行相反的步骤以继续编写您自己的代码。

那么，为什么 PR 审查不能在您可以与您自己的环境一起运行的独立环境中进行呢？

剧透警报：它可以。 这就是为什么今天我很高兴分享 CodeSandbox GitHub 应用程序🎉

在这篇文章中，我们将介绍如何使用 GitHub 应用程序进行可编辑的部署预览、可分支的数据库和端到端测试。

云开发环境最有价值的好处之一是您可以在 URL 后面运行整个项目。 您的代码、任务、测试、预览和其他一切都存在于该环境中，访问它的每个人都将获得完全相同的体验，无需进行任何设置。

与其他 GitHub 集成不同， CodeSandbox GitHub 应用程序在强大的Micro VM中运行每个分支和 PR。 这意味着无论项目多么复杂，您都可以运行任何东西，并添加 Docker、服务器和数据库。

每个 PR 的实时开发环境

通过在您的存储库中安装我们的 GitHub 应用程序，您的所有 PR 将自动在我们的一个 Micro VM 中运行。 然后，该应用程序会自动在每个 PR 中添加一个带有三个链接的评论：一个在 CodeSandbox Web 编辑器中打开代码，另一个在 VS Code 上打开它（连接到 CodeSandbox），最后另一个打开预览。

有了这个确实加快了公关审查过程。 在 URL 后面运行 PR 的好处在于，您无需切换上下文或启动任何内容即可查看 PR。 您只需单击它，查看代码、测试和预览，进行任何必要的更改并合并它。

由于每个 PR 都在功能强大的 CodeSandbox Micro VM 上持续运行，因此您可以从任何设备访问它，无论其规格如何，即使对于非常复杂的项目也是如此。 你在路上吗？ 然后在我们的iOS 应用中打开环境！ 在笔记本电脑之间来回切换？ 它将在每个人中运行完全相同。 虚拟机完成所有繁重的工作，因此无论如何您都可以获得一流的性能。

此外，由于默认情况下此环境是协作的，因此访问它的任何人都将获得完全相同的体验，并且可以实时协作，而不管他们用于访问代码的设备或编辑器如何。

因此，如果您使用我们的 扩展程序从 VS Code 访问它，而其他人使用 CodeSandbox iOS 应用程序从他们的 iPad 访问它，您可以无缝协作，就像您在同一个本地环境中编码一样。 这种协作非常有助于为您的代码提供一些上下文，或者只是与其他人一起审查它。

几个月前，当我们率先推出这项技术时，我们向早期采用者提供了它以查看它所得到的反应，我们对反应感到兴奋——它通过使 PR 审查过程更加顺畅，帮助开发人员每周节省几个小时。 看到它对开源维护者和开发团队的影响令人惊讶。

我个人认为 Adverity 团队的这句话说明了一切：

我查看了一个 PR，与 Bartosz Wisniewski 一起进行了一些更改，现场预览并推送了它们。 所有这些都无需在本地检查任何内容。 这可能现在为我节省了 1 小时。 巨大的胜利！ 通过可编辑的部署预览为整个团队赋能

虽然 git 在开发人员中无处不在，但对于设计师、经理和营销人员等其他角色可能无法访问。 然而，我已经数不清有多少次我向其他团队的同事请教我的设计、结构或工作副本的反馈。 此外，让经理了解正在完成的工作总是很重要的。

这就是 GitHub App PR 预览真正大放异彩的地方。

由于内存快照，CodeSandbox 预览是即时的。 无需等待构建完成——任何想要查看代码预览的人都可以前往 PR 并单击“预览”URL。 为了让任何人都能轻松做出贡献，我们将这些预览设为可编辑。 因此，任何看到预览的人都可以自主进行简单的设计或复制更新，并通过单击提交这些更改。 请参阅下面的协作流程：

此外，我们的预览版不仅限于静态应用程序——我们支持后端服务器、数据库以及几乎可以在 Docker 上运行的任何东西。 这使得设置部署预览变得非常容易。 就像使用docker-compose之类的东西来设置您的开发环境一样简单，然后就可以自动获得部署预览。

可分支的数据库

正如我之前提到的，因为我们在 Micro VM 中运行您的代码，所以您几乎可以运行任何东西，包括数据库。 当您在存储库的main分支中启动数据库时，每个新 PR 都会从main分支获取该数据库的精确副本。 通过这种方式，您可以轻松地在 PR 中测试架构更改，而无需在您自己的开发环境中回滚或迁移。

此外，每个 PR 都将拥有来自main分支的所有种子数据，因为它是一个精确的副本。 因此，如果您在main分支的数据库中添加一些数据，每个 PR 都将拥有可用的种子数据！

以下是Tellie在他们自己的工作流程中使用它的方式：

可分支数据库让 Tellie 的工程师可以在其所有分支机构中重复使用高保真测试数据。 通过简单地在 CodeSandbox 上使用我们的应用程序，所有工程师都可以立即使用生成的数据，而我们的操作工作量为零。 端到端测试

使用我们的 GitHub 应用程序，我们还为每个拉取请求的每个部署预览创建一个 GitHub 部署。 您可以获取此部署的 URL 并针对它运行端到端测试。 这样，您可以针对 CodeSandbox 为 PR 运行的开发服务器运行测试。

你甚至可以配置 CodeSandbox 为每个 PR 启动一个生产服务器，连接到分支的数据库。 这样，您可以在使用生产环境时针对唯一的数据库自动运行测试。

增强 git 工作流程

在 CodeSandbox，我们致力于让每个人都能更轻松地进行开发。

我们非常感谢社区提供的宝贵反馈，并在看到它对早期采用者产生的积极影响后推出我们的 GitHub 应用程序而激动不已。

我们不仅通过改进 PR 审查流程为开发人员节省了宝贵的时间，而且我们还允许其他团队的同事了解代码并能够直接在代码库上进行小的更改。

我们一直在 CodeSandbox 使用它，团队再也离不开它了！

如果您想尝试一下，可以轻松地将其安装在自己的仓库中！ 查看我们的文档或观看我们的视频教程。



原文:

We are announcing the CodeSandbox GitHub App, which gives you a live development environment for every pull request you open, with support for Docker and databases.

Remember the last time someone asked you to review a PR? You probably had to commit your current work, switch your whole context and spin everything up so you could review it. Then, do the opposite steps to keep on working on your own code.

Git is core to the day-to-day of any developer. So why can't a PR review happen on a standalone environment that you can just run alongside your own?

Spoiler alert: it can. And this is why today I'm excited to share the CodeSandbox GitHub App 🎉

In this post, we'll go through how you can use the GitHub App for editable deployment previews, brancheable databases and end-to-end tests.

CodeSandbox GitHub App

One of the most valuable benefits of cloud development environments is that you can run an entire project behind a URL. Your code, tasks, tests, previews and everything else lives inside that environment, and everyone accessing it will get the exact same experience, with no setup needed.

Unlike other GitHub integrations out there, the CodeSandbox GitHub App runs every branch and PR in powerful Micro VMs. This means you can run anything, no matter how complex the project, and add Docker, servers and databases.

A live dev environment for every PR

By installing our GitHub App in your repo, all your PRs will automatically run in one of our Micro VMs. Then, the app automatically places a comment in each PR with three links: one to open the code in the CodeSandbox web editor, another to open it on VS Code (connected to CodeSandbox), and finally another to open a preview.

Having this in place really speeds up the PR review process. The beauty of having PRs running behind a URL is that you don't have to switch your context or spin anything up in order to review the PR. You just click it, see the code, tests and a preview, go make any necessary changes, and merge it.

Because each PR is continuously running on a powerful CodeSandbox Micro VM, you can access it from any device, regardless of its specs, even for very complex projects. Are you on the go? Then open the environment in our iOS app! Switching back and forth between laptops? It will run exactly the same in every one of them. The VM does all the heavy lifting, so you get top-notch performance no matter what.

Plus, because this environment is collaborative by default, anyone accessing it will get the exact same experience and can collaborate live regardless of the device or editor that they are using to access the code.

So, if you're accessing it from VS Code using our extension and someone else is accessing it from their iPad using the CodeSandbox iOS App, you can collaborate seamlessly, as if you were coding in the same local environment. This collaboration is really helpful to provide some context behind your code or just review it live with someone else.

When we pioneered this technology a few months ago, we made it available to early adopters to see the reaction it got, and we're thrilled by the response—it's helping developers save hours every week by making the PR review process much smoother. It's amazing to see the impact it has on open source maintainers and development teams.

I personally think that this quote from the team at Adverity says it all:

I looked at a PR, made some changes together with Bartosz Wisniewski, previewed them live and pushed them. All without checking out anything locally. This probably saved me 1 hour right now. HUUGE win! — Dominik Dorfmeister

Empowering the whole team with editable deployment previews

While git is ubiquitous among developers, it might not be accessible for other roles such as designers, managers and marketers. Yet, I have lost count of how many times I have asked colleagues from other teams for feedback on the design, structure or copy of my work. Plus, it's always important to give visibility to managers over the work being done.

And here's where the GitHub App PR previews truly shine.

CodeSandbox previews are instant thanks to memory snapshotting. No need to wait for the build to finish—anyone wanting to see a preview of the code can just go to the PR and click the “Preview” URL. To empower anyone to contribute easily, we made these previews editable. So, anyone seeing a preview can make simple design or copy updates autonomously and commit these changes with a click. See this collaborative flow in action below:

Plus, our previews are not limited to static applications—we support backend servers, databases and pretty much anything you can run on Docker. This makes it very easy to set up deployment previews. It's as simple as using something like docker-compose to set up your development environment once, and have deployment previews automatically available from then on.

Brancheable databases

As I mentioned earlier, because we run your code in a Micro VM, you can run literally anything, including databases. When you start a database in the main branch of your repository, every new PR will get an exact copy of that database from the main branch. This way you can easily test schema changes inside a PR without having to roll back or migrate in your own development environment.

Also, every PR will have all the seed data from the main branch, because it's an exact copy. So if you add some data to the database in the main branch, every PR will have that seed data available!

Here's how Tellie is using this in their own workflow:

Brancheable databases let engineers at Tellie reuse high-fidelity test data across all of their branches. By simply using our application on CodeSandbox, the resulting data is immediately available to all engineers, with zero operational effort on our part. — Shree Chowkwale

End-to-end tests

With our GitHub App, we also create a GitHub Deployment for every deployment preview of every pull request. You can take the URL of this deployment and run end-to-end tests against it. This way, you can run tests against the dev server that CodeSandbox is running for the PR.

You can even configure CodeSandbox to start a production server for every PR, connected to the database of the branch. That way, you can automatically run your tests against a unique database while using a production environment.

Supercharging git workflows

At CodeSandbox, we're all about making development more accessible to everyone.

We're super thankful to the community for the valuable feedback and thrilled to launch our GitHub App after seeing the positive impact it had on its early adopters.

Not only are we saving developers precious time by improving the PR review flow, but we are also allowing colleagues from other teams to get visibility on the code and be able to make small changes directly on the codebase.

We use it all the time at CodeSandbox and the team can't live without it anymore!

If you'd like to try it out, you can install it on your own repo easily! Take a look at our docs or see our video tutorial.
